title: Knowledge Compiler: Structured Knowledge Extraction from Textbooks
oneLiner: Convert authoritative textbooks into reusable structured knowledge for trustworthy AI evaluation.
keyTakeaways: Extracted 2,306 structured knowledge objects from ACSM's Guidelines for Exercise Testing and Prescription (12th ed.) — spanning 14 ontology types including Concepts, Recommendations, Thresholds, Procedures, and Warnings.
Full pipeline reproducibility: chunk → extract → normalize → validate, with automated quality gates at each stage.
Generalized to NSCA's Essentials of Strength Training and Conditioning (5th ed.): 1,598 objects across 26 chapters, demonstrating domain-agnostic pipeline transfer.
Validation report confirms semantic fidelity: 91.2% structural accuracy against textbook source material.
Pipeline cost: ~$0.003 per object using structured LLM extraction with schema enforcement.

faq.q: Why compile textbooks into structured knowledge?
faq.a: Structured knowledge enables downstream systems (like CheckMyCoach's calibration pipeline) to verify LLM claims against a verifiable ground-truth source, rather than relying on the LLM's internal knowledge.

14 object types organized in a three-layer schema: Core Concepts (Concept, Evidence, Formula), Operational Rules (Recommendation, Threshold, Procedure, DecisionRule), and Safety (Warning, Contraindication, RiskFactor), plus supporting types (Figure, Table, TableRow).

faq.q: Can this pipeline work for other textbooks?
faq.a: Yes — the pipeline is textbook-agnostic. Schema and prompts are the main per-book adaptation cost. Demonstrated by successful ACSM → NSCA transfer.

reproducible.quickStart: git clone https://github.com/MaxGuo1220/acsms12-manifest && cd acsms12-manifest && pip install -r requirements.txt && python -m compiler.cli run --book acsm12

changelog.event: NSCA CSCS 5th edition complete: 1,598 objects, 26 chapters
Pipeline v2: chunk → extract → normalize → validate with quality gates
Initial schema design and pilot extraction (15 objects)
